steps.action: Launch QEMU with full display pipeline
steps.observed: System booted successfully, all 4 SMP cores online
Clean serial output - fonts loaded, display pipeline running, no errors
Title bar shows 'Text' label, document icon, time '21:06:24'. Cursor visible in document area. No corruption.

steps.expected: W and i have different advance widths
steps.observed: Test passed: advance('W') != advance('i') confirmed for proportional font

steps.expected: Shaping W vs i produces different advances

steps.observed: 15 characters visible: 'helloworld1test'. Space key not rendered (QEMU sendkey syntax issue), shift-1 produced '1' not '!'
15 characters visible in document area, rendered cleanly with no corruption

steps.action: Check serial output for panics during typing
steps.observed: Serial output clean - no panics, errors, or faults during input

assertions.title: Graceful degradation on font load failure

steps.action: Kill existing QEMU and clean up

steps.observed: Both fonts renamed, only source-code-pro.ttf remains
System booted. Serial shows '9p: walk error 2', 'file not found', 'prop font read failed' but continued booting
Clean serial - system reached 'display pipeline running' and 'init - running (idle)'
Title bar rendered with 'Text' label and time using monospace font. Cursor visible. No visual corruption.
Both nunito-sans.ttf and nunito-sans-variable.ttf restored, verified with ls

evidence.network: 9p walk error 2 for nunito-sans.ttf (expected - file removed)

steps.action: Boot QEMU with full font pipeline
steps.observed: Serial confirms: 9p loaded source-code-pro.ttf (9436 bytes) and nunito-sans.ttf (141600 bytes), shared memory allocated, compositor rasterized fonts, core entered event loop, display pipeline running
15 characters ('helloworld1test') rendered on screen through full pipeline: init→9p→shared memory→core→scene graph→compositor→GPU

Serial confirms: init loads fonts via 9p, shared memory scene graph allocated, compositor starts with 'monospace font rasterized' and 'proportional font rasterized', core starts with 'font metrics loaded', enters render loop
Screenshot shows 'helloworld1test' rendered cleanly with cursor, title bar with doc icon and time clock

evidence.network: 9p file reads: source-code-pro.ttf (9436 bytes), nunito-sans.ttf (141600 bytes), test.png (884951 bytes), doc-icon.svg (133 bytes), img-icon.svg (173 bytes)

frictions.description: QEMU sendkey 'space' does not produce a space character in the OS — the text shows 'helloworld1test' without spaces. QEMU monitor may need 'spc' instead of 'space', or the OS input driver may not map the space scancode correctly.
frictions.resolution: Not investigated further — core assertion (text appears, no corruption) still passes regardless of space key behavior

frictions.description: QEMU sendkey 'shift-1' produces '1' instead of '!' — modifier key combinations may need different syntax in QEMU monitor protocol
frictions.resolution: Not investigated — does not affect core assertion pass/fail

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. VAL-E2E-001 confirmed shaped text visible at boot with clean serial and complementary unit tests passing (W vs i advance widths differ). VAL-E2E-002 confirmed 15 typed characters render without corruption. VAL-E2E-003 confirmed graceful fallback to monospace when proportional font missing. VAL-CROSS-001 confirmed full font-to-pixel pipeline (9p→shared memory→core→scene graph→compositor→GPU). Font files restored after VAL-E2E-003 test.
